doc.title: Pension Expense with PSC Amortization
doc.event: Marlow Inc.'s defined benefit plan reports for the year: service cost $60,000, interest on the PBO $39,000, expected return on plan assets $24,000, and amortization of prior service cost $12,000. Marlow contributed $70,000 to the plan. Prepare the year-end entry to record pension expense, the OCI reclassification, and funding.

lines.trap: PSC amortization ADDS to expense — it's deferred cost finally flowing through income.

lines.why: Amortization pulls PSC OUT of accumulated OCI and into expense — so OCI—PSC is credited (reduced).
lines.trap: Students want to debit this. The debit happened at the amendment; amortization reverses it piece by piece.

lines.why: Plug: 87,000 − 12,000 − 70,000 = 5,000 growth in the net liability.

memorize.body: Amortizing PSC = credit OCI—Prior Service Cost, debit (inside) Pension Expense. The AOCI balance drains as expense picks it up.
S-I-R-P-G order for expense: Service, Interest, Return (minus), PSC amort, Gain/loss amort.

questions.prompt: SC $60,000 · IC $39,000 · expected return $24,000 · PSC amortization $12,000. Pension expense = ?

distractors.feedback: You dropped the PSC amortization. Prior service cost granted earlier flows into THIS period's expense: 75,000 + 12,000 = 87,000.
You added the expected return instead of subtracting it. Plan earnings reduce expense.
That's the cash contribution. Expense is built from the actuarial components, not the check written.

build.scaffold: Four credits max: OCI—PSC for the amortization, Cash for the contribution, Pension Asset/Liability for the plug.
